name: decord (Efficient Video Frame Reader for ML)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); DML / Amazon Web Services

performance.notes: C++ FFmpeg backend; random access to frames; GPU decoding via NVDEC; 5x faster than OpenCV for random access

agent_readiness.notes: Fast video frame reader optimized for ML training data loading. from decord import VideoReader, gpu, cpu. vr = VideoReader('video.mp4', ctx=cpu(0)). len(vr). vr[0].asnumpy() — first frame as numpy. vr.get_batch([0, 30, 60]).asnumpy() — random access batch. vr[::10].asnumpy() — every 10th frame. GPU decode: vr = VideoReader('video.mp4', ctx=gpu(0)). AVReader for audio+video. WebDataset integration. PyTorch DataLoader: VideoLoader. 5x faster than OpenCV for random frame access. Used in video ML training pipelines.

gotchas: Binary wheels only for CPU; GPU requires CUDA compilation from source
Maintenance has been less active than OpenCV
Seeking accuracy can be imprecise for some codecs
Returns decord.NDArray (not NumPy) — call .asnumpy() for conversion
best_when: Video ML dataset loading in PyTorch/TensorFlow DataLoaders
Random frame access for video classification/recognition training
Batch frame extraction for feature computation
When OpenCV's sequential decode is too slow for training
summary: decord is an efficient video reader for ML training data loading. VideoReader('video.mp4', ctx=cpu(0)). vr.get_batch([0, 30, 60]).asnumpy() for random frame access. vr[::10] for strided sampling. GPU decoding via NVDEC. 5x faster than OpenCV for random access. PyTorch DataLoader integration. Apache-2.0. Best for video ML pipelines requiring fast random-access frame loading. Returns decord.NDArray — call .asnumpy() for NumPy.
